title: Cyclobalanopsis kamroopii
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis kamroopii is a species of evergreen tree in the family Fagaceae. This tree is native to the Himalayan foothills and is commonly found in the mountainous regions of northern India, Nepal, and Bhutan. As a member of the Fagaceae family, it is closely related to other oak species. The Fagaceae family is further classified under the order Fagales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes beech and chestnut trees. Cyclobalanopsis kamroopii is a part of the genus Cyclobalanopsis, which includes several other species of evergreen oaks native to Asia.
uses: Cyclobalanopsis kamroopii has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are believed to help with wound healing and infections. In addition, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. The leaves of the plant are also used to make tea, which is said to have antioxidant properties and help lower blood pressure.
